generated_by: interactive wrap-up — v1 conversational walk, conducted manually as the first dogfood of architecture/interactive-wrapup-spec.md
outcome_lane_label: works, but not what I asked for (Discovery misalignment)

verdict_cross_check.reading: Orthogonal. A build can pass verification and still miss intent; Lane B is Discovery misalignment by definition, so the recorded verdict does not contradict the user's answer.

divergence.delivered: The app returns the closest wine shop.
divergence.user_detail: It shows a wine shop; I wanted a liquor store.

culprit.decision_text: 'Liquor store' means a retail business that sells packaged alcoholic beverages (a category, not a specific named chain).
culprit.explanation: A4 flattened a jurisdiction-defined legal category into one universal definition. What legally counts as a 'liquor store', and what such a store may sell, is set by local alcohol law and varies by jurisdiction. TD faithfully implemented A4 — IP1's resolution to OpenStreetMap shop=alcohol is precisely A4's definition — so the implementation is not the bug; the assumption is.

compounding_factor.decision_text: Use the OpenStreetMap Overpass API for shop=alcohol features near the user.
compounding_factor.note: shop=alcohol is a single global, jurisdiction-agnostic tag. It cannot represent local legal store categories, so even a corrected A4 could not be honored by this data source without a different approach.

called_shot.note: A4's own what_breaks_if_wrong read: 'Edge regions where liquor store maps to a different store type (e.g., grocery with alcohol) could surface unexpected results.' The architecture flagged this exact failure mode — at high confidence — and proceeded on the assumption anyway.

corrected_intent: 'Liquor store' is a jurisdiction-defined legal category. The build needed to resolve the user's locale and its alcohol laws — or surface the jurisdiction-dependence as an inflection point — rather than logging a universal high-confidence assumption.
amendment_candidate: When a prompt uses a term whose meaning is set by local law or jurisdiction (liquor store, pharmacy, notary, ...), Discovery must not log a single universal high-confidence assumption for it. It must surface the jurisdiction-dependence as an inflection point grounded in the user's locale; and TD must verify the chosen data source can represent the jurisdiction-specific distinction.

ab_rerun_input.note: Re-run with the corrected assumption pinned ('liquor store' resolved per the user's jurisdiction) to confirm A4 is the real culprit — or re-run the verbatim prompt after the amendment lands, as the clean A/B.

lifecycle.note: Discovery misalignment is a Phase-1 documented gap, not a Phase 2 trigger (build-lifecycle.md). first_delivery_outcome is untouched.
